name: Excretory Products and their Elimination
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Excretory Products and their Elimination

tests.testPaperName: Excretory Products And Their Elimination Test-01
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Excretory Products and their Elimination

questions.question: Blood enters glomerular capillaries through ________ arteriole & leaves through ________ arteriole.
questions.question_raw: Blood enters glomerular capillaries through ________ arteriole & leaves through ________ arteriole.
questions.explanation: The blood plasma is filtered through the capillaries of the glomerulus into the capsule. The Bowman's capsule empties the filtrate into the proximal tubule that is also part of the duct system of the nephron. A glomerulus receives its blood supply from an afferent arteriole of the renal circulation.

questions.question: A renal corpuscle is:
questions.question_raw: A renal corpuscle is:
questions.explanation: Renal corpuscle, also called malpighian body, filtration unit of vertebrate nephrons, functional units of the kidney. It consists of a knot of capillaries (glomerulus) surrounded by a double-walled capsule (Bowman's capsule) that opens into a tubule.

questions.question: Homeostasis in the body is largely maintained by:
questions.question_raw: Homeostasis in the body is largely maintained by:
questions.explanation: Homeostasis is the maintenance of constant body temperature. It is mainly maintained by excretion and osmoregulation, which is the function of the kidney.

questions.question: The urinary bladder is absent in:
questions.question_raw: The urinary bladder is absent in:
questions.explanation: Urinary bladder is absent in snake and bird because the urine of these is semi-solid consisting mainly of uric acid. The primary function of the urinary bladder is to store urine in mammals. The urinary bladder cannot store semi-solid urine.

questions.question: Ascending limb of Henle’s loop is not permeable for:
questions.question_raw: Ascending limb of Henle’s loop is not permeable for:
questions.explanation: Ascending limb of Henle’s is not permeable to water. The descending limb is although permeable to water. Countercurrent exchange takes place between the loop of Henle’s and arteries surrounding it.

questions.question: The physiological unit of a kidney is:
questions.question_raw: The physiological unit of a kidney is:
questions.explanation: Nephrons are the physical unit or functional unit of the kidney. Millions of nephrons are present in each kidney but a physiological unit of the kidney is uriniferous tubules.

questions.question: The adult human kidney is metanephric. So, first embryonic tubules formed in vertebrates during embryonic development are:
questions.question_raw: The adult human kidney is metanephric. So, first embryonic tubules formed in vertebrates during embryonic development are:
questions.explanation: The adult human kidney is metanephric, the third and final excretory organ that develops in a vertebrate embryo. First embryonic tubules formed in vertebrates during embryonic development are pronephric.

questions.question: Urinary bladder helps to:
questions.question_raw: Urinary bladder helps to:

options.label: A
options.text: Stores urine for reabsorbing water from it

questions.explanation: The urinary bladder is a hollow elastic organ that functions as the body's urine storage tank. Urine produced by the kidneys flows through the ureters to the urinary bladder, where is it stored before passing into the urethra and exiting the body.

questions.question: The part of a nephron which adds some material to the filtrate is:
questions.question_raw: The part of a nephron which adds some material to the filtrate is:
questions.explanation: Each nephron consists of Bowman’s cup, tubules, and loops of Henle’s. The filtration of blood occurs in glomerulus cells present in Bowman’s cup. Some materials are added to the initial filtrate in the distal convoluted tubule.

questions.question:
In the human kidney:
 the outer zone is called the medulla and inner zone is called the cortex.
 cortex extends between medullary pyramids as renal columns of Bellini.
questions.question_raw:
In the human kidney:

  1. the outer zone is called the medulla and inner zone is called the cortex.
  2. cortex extends between medullary pyramids as renal columns of Bellini.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (ii) is wrong and (i) is correct

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is incorrect

questions.explanation: Inside the kidney, there are two zones, an outer cortex, and an inner medulla. The cortex extends in between the medullary pyramids as renal columns called columns of Bertini.

questions.question: Vasa recta is absent or reduced in:
questions.question_raw: Vasa recta is absent or reduced in:
questions.explanation: The efferent arteriole emerging from the glomerulus forms a fine capillary network around the renal tubule called the peritubular capillaries. A minute vessel of this network runs parallel to Henle’s loop forming a ‘U’ shaped vasa recta. Vasa recta are absent or highly reduced in cortical nephrons.

questions.question: Towards the centre of the inner concave surface of the kidney is a notch called:
questions.question_raw: Towards the centre of the inner concave surface of the kidney is a notch called:
questions.explanation: On the medial margin of the kidney is a concave region called the renal hilum. The renal hilum is the entrance to the renal sinus. Structures such as the renal veins, artery, nerves, and lymphatic vessels are located in the renal hilum.

questions.question: Label the following in diagram
questions.question_raw: Label the following in diagram
questions.explanation: The diagram shown above is Malpighian body or renal corpuscles. The marked part in this is called Podocytes or visceral epithelial cells in Bowman’s capsule in the kidney that warp around capillaries of the glomerules.

questions.question:
In the human excretory system:
 Kidneys and ureters are paired structures but the urinary bladder is single.
 Kidneys are situated between the levels of the last thoracic and third lumbar vertebra.
questions.question_raw:
In the human excretory system:

  1. Kidneys and ureters are paired structures but the urinary bladder is single.
  2. Kidneys are situated between the levels of the last thoracic and third lumbar vertebra.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

questions.explanation: The excretory system in humans consists of a pair of kidneys, one pair of ureters, a urinary bladder, and a urethra. Kidneys are reddish-brown, bean-shaped structures situated between the levels of the last thoracic and third lumbar vertebra close to the dorsal inner wall of the abdominal cavity.

questions.question: In the following diagram what are 7,9 & 10 :
questions.question_raw: In the following diagram what are 7,9 & 10 :
questions.explanation: Towards the centre of the innerconcave surface of the kidney is a notchcalled hilum through which ureter, bloodvessels and nerves enter. Inner to the hilumis a broad funnel shaped space called therenal pelvis with projections called calyces. The medulla is divided into a few conical masses projecting into the calyces. Thus, 7, 9, and 10 are pelvis, hilum and calyx respectively.

tests.testPaperName: Excretory Products And Their Elimination Test-02
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Excretory Products and their Elimination

questions.question: Blood enters glomerular capillaries through ________ arteriole and leaves through ________ arteriole.
questions.question_raw: Blood enters glomerular capillaries through ________ arteriole and leaves through ________ arteriole.
questions.explanation: Bowman cup contains glomerular capillaries to filter the blood. Blood enters the glomerular capillaries through afferent arteriole and leaves through the efferent arteriole.

questions.question: pH of urine under healthy conditions is:
questions.question_raw: pH of urine under healthy conditions is:
questions.explanation: The pH of urine under healthy conditions is slightly less than 7 so, slightly acidic in nature.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of glomerular filtrate?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a part of glomerular filtrate?
questions.explanation: Glomerular filtrate contains glucose, inorganic salts, and creatinine but it does not contain globulins protein.

questions.question: The epithelial cells of Bowman’s capsule are called:
questions.question_raw: The epithelial cells of Bowman’s capsule are called:
questions.explanation: Podocytes are cells in the Bowman's capsule in the kidneys that wrap around capillaries of the glomerulus. The Bowman's capsule filters the blood, retaining large molecules such as proteins while smaller molecules such as water, salts, and sugars are filtered as the first step in the formation of urine.

questions.question: Conditional reabsorption of sodium ions and water takes place in_______.
questions.question_raw: Conditional reabsorption of sodium ions and water takes place in_______.
questions.explanation: Distal Convoluted Tubule (DCT): Conditional reabsorption of Na^+ and water takes place in this segment. DCT is also capable of reabsorption of HCO_3^- and selective secretion of hydrogen and potassium ions and NH_3 to maintain the pH and sodium-potassium balance in the blood.

questions.question: The filtration fraction is the ratio of GFR to RPF where both the values are in ml/min and FF is expressed in percentage. Calculate FF for a normal adult human being, if RPF= 600ml/min:
questions.question_raw: The filtration fraction is the ratio of GFR to RPF where both the values are in ml/min and FF is expressed in percentage. Calculate FF for a normal adult human being, if RPF= 600ml/min:
questions.explanation:
Filtration fraction is the ratio of glomerular filtration rate (GFR) to the renal plasma flow (RPF). GFR of a normal human is 120 ml/min. so,
FF = ${GFR\over RPF} \times 100$
= ${120\over 600} \times 100$ = 20%.

questions.question: During urine formation, the tubular cells secrete ________ into the filtrate.
questions.question_raw: During urine formation, the tubular cells secrete ________ into the filtrate.

options.label: D
options.text: hydrogen ions, potassium ions and ammonia

correct_option_texts: hydrogen ions, potassium ions and ammonia
questions.explanation:
The substances that are secreted into the tubular fluid (for removal from the body) include:
 Potassium ions (K+),
 Hydrogen ions (H+),
 Ammonium ions (NH_4+),
 Creatinine,
 Urea,
 Some hormones,
 and some drugs (e.g. penicillin).

questions.question:
Counter current mechanism:
 It operates in all vertebrates.
 It produces hypertonic urine.
questions.question_raw:
Counter current mechanism:

  1. It operates in all vertebrates.
  2. It produces hypertonic urine.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct
questions.explanation: Countercurrent mechanism operates in mammals but not in all vertebrates. Due to the countercurrent mechanism, it produces hypertonic urine, highly concentrated.

questions.question:
Read the following statements about urine formation:
 Ultrafiltration doesn’t need any energy expenditure by kidney cells.
 Selective reabsorption of glucose, water, urea, hormones, amino acids, etc takes place through passive transport.
 PCT cells have many microvilli along with few mitochondria since most of the nutrients are absorbed passively.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements about urine formation:

  1. Ultrafiltration doesn’t need any energy expenditure by kidney cells.
  2. Selective reabsorption of glucose, water, urea, hormones, amino acids, etc takes place through passive transport.
  3. PCT cells have many microvilli along with few mitochondria since most of the nutrients are absorbed passively.
questions.explanation: During urine formation, ultrafiltration takes place in which energy is not required by cells of the kidney. Selective reabsorption of glucose, water, salt, etc. occurs actively and proximal convoluted tubules (PCT) do not have microvilli.

questions.question: If in glomerular capillaries, pressure of blood is 75 mm Hg, osmotic pressure of plasma proteins is 30 mm Hg, and hydrostatic pressure of fluid in renal tubule is 25 mm Hg then net force or the filtration pressure will be calculated as :
questions.question_raw: If in glomerular capillaries, pressure of blood is 75 mm Hg, osmotic pressure of plasma proteins is 30 mm Hg, and hydrostatic pressure of fluid in renal tubule is 25 mm Hg then net force or the filtration pressure will be calculated as :
questions.explanation: Net force of filtration pressure is equal to the blood pressure of glomerular capillaries minus sum of the osmotic pressure of plasma and hydrostatic pressure of fluid in renal tubule. So, filtration pressure = 75 – [ 30 + 20 ] mm Hg.

questions.question: The hormone with enzymatic action which catalyses the conversion of angiotensinogen into angiotensin is:
questions.question_raw: The hormone with enzymatic action which catalyses the conversion of angiotensinogen into angiotensin is:
questions.explanation: A fall in glomerular blood flow/glomerular blood pressure/GFR can activate the JG cells to release renin which converts angiotensinogen in the blood to angiotensin I and further to angiotensin II.

questions.question: Vasopressin is a hormone involved in the regulation of urine formation. It is secreted from:
questions.question_raw: Vasopressin is a hormone involved in the regulation of urine formation. It is secreted from:
questions.explanation: An excessive loss of fluid from the body can activate receptors that stimulate the hypothalamus to release antidiuretic hormone (ADH) or vasopressin from the neurohypophysis.

questions.question: Select the mismatched pair of the following:
questions.question_raw: Select the mismatched pair of the following:

options.label: A
options.text: Cortical Nephron - About 25% of total nephrons

options.label: B
options.text: Glomerular Filtration Rate - 180 litres per day

options.label: D
options.text: Juxtamedullary Nephron - About 15% of total nephrons

questions.explanation: A cortical nephron is about 25% of total nephrons. The glomerular filtration rate is 180 litre per day. Juxtamedullary nephron makes about 15% of total nephrons but the lung does not remove 180 L of carbon dioxide per day.

questions.question: What are the unlabeled a, b & c in the given flow chart?
questions.question_raw: What are the unlabeled a, b & c in the given flow chart?
questions.explanation: Renin convert angiotensin I to angiotensin II which activates adrenal cortex to release aldosterone. Aldosterone helps to reabsorption of sodium and water from DCT.

questions.question:
Read the following statements about renal threshold:
 The renal threshold of glucose is 180 mg/100ml of blood.
 If the blood level of glucose exceeds 180mg, it leads to diabetes insipidus.
 If the blood level of glucose exceeds 180mg, it leads to diabetes mellitus.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements about renal threshold:

  1. The renal threshold of glucose is 180 mg/100ml of blood.
  2. If the blood level of glucose exceeds 180mg, it leads to diabetes insipidus.
  3. If the blood level of glucose exceeds 180mg, it leads to diabetes mellitus.
questions.explanation: The renal threshold is the state when glucose is present in urine. The renal threshold of glucose is 180 mg/100 ml of blood. If the blood glucose level exceeds 180 mg, it leads to diabetes insipidus.

tests.testPaperName: Excretory Products And Their Elimination Test-03
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Excretory Products and their Elimination

questions.question: During micturition, the muscles of urinary bladder, and urethral sphincters will:
questions.question_raw: During micturition, the muscles of urinary bladder, and urethral sphincters will:
questions.explanation: The CNS passes on motor messages to initiate the contraction of smooth muscles of the bladder and simultaneous relaxation of the urethral sphincter causing the release of urine.

questions.question: In the following pictorial representation of Kreb’s Hensleit cycle, find out 1,2& 3?
questions.question_raw: In the following pictorial representation of Kreb’s Hensleit cycle, find out 1,2& 3?
questions.explanation: Kreb’s Hensleit cycle is the sequence of chemical reaction occurring primarily in the liver that results in the production of urea. In this cycle ammonia is fist converted into citrulline along with carbon dioxide that change into arginine and finally into ornithine.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 Infants and toddlers are often under the habit of bed wetting since micturition is a reflex process.
 Adults and grown-up children can control this reflex process, voluntarily to some extent.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. Infants and toddlers are often under the habit of bed wetting since micturition is a reflex process.
  2. Adults and grown-up children can control this reflex process, voluntarily to some extent.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

questions.explanation: The process of release of urine is called micturition and the neural mechanism causing it is called the micturition reflex. Adults and grown-up children can control the reflex process of micturition voluntarily to some extent.

questions.question: All of the following organs help in excretion except:
questions.question_raw: All of the following organs help in excretion except:
questions.explanation: The lungs help in the excretion of carbon dioxide, the liver help in the excretion of the toxic substance, and the skin help in the removal of extra salts along with sweat. The heart is the organ that pumps the blood in the body.

questions.question: Urea cycle operates in:
questions.question_raw: Urea cycle operates in:
questions.explanation: Urea cycles operate in the liver, where ammonia is converted into urea. Sometimes urea cycle also occurs in the kidney.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 Avian kidneys have very short loops of Henle.
 Birds are unable to make urine as hypotonic as mammals can.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. Avian kidneys have very short loops of Henle.
  2. Birds are unable to make urine as hypotonic as mammals can.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct
questions.explanation: Avian kidneys have very short loops of Henle’s. Loops of Henle’s used in the concentration of urine. Birds make urine highly hypotonic.

questions.question: If kidney tissues are supplied with blood low in oxygen then:
questions.question_raw: If kidney tissues are supplied with blood low in oxygen then:

options.label: A
options.text: Both tubular reabsorption and ultrafiltration stops

options.label: C
options.text: Both tubular reabsorption and ultrafiltration continue normally

correct_option_texts: Both tubular reabsorption and ultrafiltration stops
questions.explanation: Both tubular reabsorption and ultrafiltration stops

questions.question: Diabetes insipidus is caused due to:
questions.question_raw: Diabetes insipidus is caused due to:
questions.explanation: Diabetes insipidus is a rate variety of diabetes characterized by excessive thrust and excretion of a large amount of urine. It is caused due to reduced secretion of antidiuretic hormone (ADH) or vasopressin hormone.

questions.question: Rahul is anaemic and a few days ago suffered from massive haemorrhage. Now, what problems he may face:
questions.question_raw: Rahul is anaemic and a few days ago suffered from massive haemorrhage. Now, what problems he may face:

options.label: B
options.text: Accumulation of waste products in body

questions.explanation: If the anaemic person suffered from massive haemorrhage, the problem he may face includes very low blood pressure, accumulation of wastes products in the body, and no glomerular filtration in the kidney.

questions.question: Reema is on dialysis and she is unable to conceive due to high levels of waste products in her body fluids. Her doctor can suggest her ________.
questions.question_raw: Reema is on dialysis and she is unable to conceive due to high levels of waste products in her body fluids. Her doctor can suggest her ________.
questions.explanation: When both kidneys of a person get damaged and not able to filter the blood, dialysis is used to remove nitrogenous wastes. High wastes accumulation is unable a woman to conceive. The ultimate solution to this problem is a kidney transplant.

questions.question: People with chronic kidney disease are usually affected by anaemia. It happens because a damaged kidney doesn’t secrete sufficient:
questions.question_raw: People with chronic kidney disease are usually affected by anaemia. It happens because a damaged kidney doesn’t secrete sufficient:
questions.explanation: The person suffering from chronic kidney disease are usually affected by anaemia. The damaged kidney is not able to secrete sufficient erythropoietin in the blood that helps in the absorption of iron.

questions.question:
Match the kidney disorders and its symptoms:
 (a) Ketosis
 (i) frequent thirst
 (b) Polydipsia
 (ii) blood in urine
 (c) Alkaptonuria
 (iii) acetoacetic acid in urine
 (d) Haematuria
 (iv) homogentisic acid in urine
questions.question_raw:
Match the kidney disorders and its symptoms:

(a) Ketosis (i) frequent thirst
(b) Polydipsia (ii) blood in urine
(c) Alkaptonuria (iii) acetoacetic acid in urine
(d) Haematuria (iv) homogentisic acid in urine
questions.explanation: Ketosis is the presence of acetoacetic acid in the urine, polydipsia is the condition of frequent thirst, alkaptonuria is the presence of homogentisic acid in urine and haematuria is the presence of blood in the urine.

questions.question: Dev consumes a large amount of alcohol and the result is polyuria and dehydration due to:
questions.question_raw: Dev consumes a large amount of alcohol and the result is polyuria and dehydration due to:

options.label: A
options.text: Increase in the level of vasopressin

options.label: B
options.text: Decrease in the level of vasopressin

options.label: C
options.text: Decrease in the level of ANF

options.label: D
options.text: Decrease in the level of aldosterone

correct_option_texts: Decrease in the level of vasopressin
questions.explanation: Polyuria is usually the result of drinking excessive amounts of fluids (polydipsia), particularly water and fluids that contain caffeine or alcohol. A person suffering from polyuria may develop excessive production of urea and suffer from dehydration due to a decrease in the level of antidiuretic hormone or vasopressin.

questions.question: Which one of the following is correct with reference to haemodialysis?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is correct with reference to haemodialysis?

options.label: A
options.text: Anti-heparin is added prior to haemodialysis.

options.label: B
options.text: It absorbs and resends excess of ions.

options.label: C
options.text: The dialysis unit has a coiled semi permeable cellophane tube.

options.label: D
options.text: Nitrogenous wastes are removed by active transport

correct_option_texts: The dialysis unit has a coiled semi permeable cellophane tube.
questions.explanation: In haemodialysis, blood drained from a convenient artery is pumped into a dialysing unit after adding an anticoagulant like heparin. The unit contains a coiled cellophane tube surrounded by a fluid (dialysing fluid) having the same composition as that of plasma except for the nitrogenous wastes. The porous cellophane membrane of the tube allows the passage of molecules based on concentration gradient. As nitrogenous wastes are absent in the dialysing fluid, these substances freely move out, thereby clearing the blood. The cleared blood is pumped back to the body through a vein after adding anti-heparin to it.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Animal
 Excretory organ
 (a) Dugesia
 (i) body surface
 (b) Palaemon
 (ii) malpighian tubules
 (c) Palamnaeus
 (iii) protonephridia
 (d) Sea anemone
 (iv) green glands
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Animal Excretory organ
(a) Dugesia (i) body surface
(b) Palaemon (ii) malpighian tubules
(c) Palamnaeus (iii) protonephridia
(d) Sea anemone (iv) green glands
questions.explanation: Excretory system of Dugesia is protonephridia, Palaemon is a body surface, Palamnaeus is malpighian tubules and that of Sea anemone is body surface.
